texts.orig: TAKE NOTICE that after thirty (30) days from the date of this Gazette, and unless cause be shown to the contrary, I intend to apply to the High Court at Kisii for representation of the estates of the persons named in the second column of the schedule hereto, who died on the dates respectively set forth against their names.
texts.text: TAKE NOTICE that after thirty (30) days from the date of this Gazette, and unless cause be shown to the contrary, I intend to apply to the High Court at Kisii for representation of the estates of the persons named in the second column of the schedule hereto, who died on the dates respectively set forth against their names.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: And further take notice that all persons having any claims against or interests in the estates of the said deceased persons are required to prove such claims or interests before me within two (2) months from the date of this Gazette, after which date the claims and interests are proved will be paid and satisfied and the several estates distributed according to law.
texts.text: And further take notice that all persons having any claims against or interests in the estates of the said deceased persons are required to prove such claims or interests before me within two (2) months from the date of this Gazette, after which date the claims and interests are proved will be paid and satisfied and the several estates distributed according to law.

texts.text: PROPOSED REGULATIONS IN RESPECT OF THE BLENDING OF POWER ALCOHOL WITH MOTOR GASOLINE

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. PURSUANT to section 110 (3) of the Energy Act , 2006, the Energy Regulatory Commission hereby invites members of the public to submit written comments within forty (40) days from the date of publication of this notice to the Director General, the Energy Regulatory Commission, on the proposed Regulations in respect of the Blending of Power Alcohol with Motor Gasoline which are set out in Part II hereunder.
texts.text: PURSUANT to section 110 (3) of the Energy Act , 2006, the Energy Regulatory Commission hereby invites members of the public to submit written comments within forty (40) days from the date of publication of this notice to the Director General, the Energy Regulatory Commission, on the proposed Regulations in respect of the Blending of Power Alcohol with Motor Gasoline which are set out in Part II hereunder.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Comments may be hand-delivered, posted, and facsimiled or e-mailed to the Energy Regulatory Commission.
texts.text: Comments may be hand-delivered, posted, and facsimiled or e-mailed to the Energy Regulatory Commission.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Energy Regulatory Commission First Floor, Integrity Centre Milimani Road Nairobi.
texts.text: The Energy Regulatory Commission First Floor, Integrity Centre Milimani Road Nairobi.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Postal Address: P.O. Box 42681 -00100 GPO Nairobi
texts.text: Postal Address: P.O. Box 42681 -00100 GPO Nairobi

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: THE ENERGY ACT (BLENDING OF POWER ALCOHOL WITH MOTOR GASOLINE) REGULATIONS, 2009
texts.text: THE ENERGY ACT (BLENDING OF POWER ALCOHOL WITH MOTOR GASOLINE) REGULATIONS, 2009

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. These regulations may be cited as the Energy Act (Blending of power alcohol with motor gasoline) Regulations, 2009.
texts.text: These regulations may be cited as the Energy Act (Blending of power alcohol with motor gasoline) Regulations, 2009.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. In these Regulations, unless the content otherwise requires-
texts.text: In these Regulations, unless the content otherwise requires-

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'power alcohol' means bioethanol meeting Kenya Standard KS 03382 or any revisions thereon.
texts.text: 'power alcohol' means bioethanol meeting Kenya Standard KS 03382 or any revisions thereon.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'motor gasoline' means  fossil fuel meeting Kenya Standard KS 2060 or any revisions thereon.
texts.text: 'motor gasoline' means  fossil fuel meeting Kenya Standard KS 2060 or any revisions thereon.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'gasohol' means the fuel made from blending power alcohol and motor gasoline to meet Kenya Standard KS 03-515: 1990 or any revisions thereon.
texts.text: 'gasohol' means the fuel made from blending power alcohol and motor gasoline to meet Kenya Standard KS 03-515: 1990 or any revisions thereon.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 'Kenya Standard' means the specification or code of practice declared by the National Standards Council under section 9 of the Standards Act, Cap. 496.
texts.text: 'Kenya Standard' means the specification or code of practice declared by the National Standards Council under section 9 of the Standards Act, Cap. 496.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. With effect from 1st March 2010, all motor gasoline loaded from the petroleum storage and loading depots indicated in Schedule 1 herewith, for sale in Kenya, shall be blended with power alcohol to make gasohol.
texts.text: With effect from 1st March 2010, all motor gasoline loaded from the petroleum storage and loading depots indicated in Schedule 1 herewith, for sale in Kenya, shall be blended with power alcohol to make gasohol.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PETROLEUM STORAGE AND LOADING DEPOTS WHERE GASOHOL BLENDING SHALL BE UNDERTAKEN
texts.text: PETROLEUM STORAGE AND LOADING DEPOTS WHERE GASOHOL BLENDING SHALL BE UNDERTAKEN

texts.label: text
texts.orig: IN EXERCISE of the powers conferred by section 41 (A) of the Constitution  of  Kenya,  and  pursuant  to  a  Court  decree  dated  11th November,  2009,  issued  under  section  61  of  the  Local  Government Act, the Interim Independent Electoral Commission gives notice that the  election  of  Faustine  Ndwiga  Gatumo  as  councillor  has  been revoked  and  in  place  thereof  Njeru  Paul  Njuki  declared  the  duly elected councillor for Gaturi South Electoral Area.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: IMPORTANT NOTICE TO SUBSCRIBERS TO THE KENYA GAZETTE
texts.text: IMPORTANT NOTICE TO SUBSCRIBERS TO THE KENYA GAZETTE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: THE following notes are for the guidance of persons submitting 'copy' for inclusion in the Kenya Gazette, Supplement , etc.:
texts.text: THE following notes are for the guidance of persons submitting 'copy' for inclusion in the Kenya Gazette, Supplement , etc.:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (1) The Kenya Gazette contains Notices of a general nature which do not affect  legislation.  They  are,  therefore,  submitted  to  the  Government Printer directly.
texts.text: The Kenya Gazette contains Notices of a general nature which do not affect  legislation.  They  are,  therefore,  submitted  to  the  Government Printer directly.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (2) Legislative  Supplement contains  Rules  and  Regulations  which  are issued  by  the  Central  Government.  Because  of  this,  they  must  be submitted  to  the  Government  Printer  through  the  office  of  the Attorney-General.
texts.text: Legislative  Supplement contains  Rules  and  Regulations  which  are issued  by  the  Central  Government.  Because  of  this,  they  must  be submitted  to  the  Government  Printer  through  the  office  of  the Attorney-General.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (3) Bill  Supplement contains  Bills  which  are  for  introduction  in  the National Assembly.
texts.text: Bill  Supplement contains  Bills  which  are  for  introduction  in  the National Assembly.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (4) Act Supplement contains Acts passed by the National Assembly.
texts.text: Act Supplement contains Acts passed by the National Assembly.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All 'copy' submitted for publication should be prepared on one side of an A4  sheet  no  matter  how  small  the  Notice  is,  each  page  being  numbered  and should be typewritten with double spacing. Copy should be clear, legible and contain no alterations.
texts.text: All 'copy' submitted for publication should be prepared on one side of an A4  sheet  no  matter  how  small  the  Notice  is,  each  page  being  numbered  and should be typewritten with double spacing. Copy should be clear, legible and contain no alterations.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Particular attention should be paid to the following points:
texts.text: Particular attention should be paid to the following points:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) Signature must be supported by rubber-stamping or typing the name of the signatory in capital letters.
texts.text: (i) Signature must be supported by rubber-stamping or typing the name of the signatory in capital letters.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (ii) Dates must be correct and filled in where necessary.
texts.text: (ii) Dates must be correct and filled in where necessary.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (iii) Care  should  be  taken  to  ensure  that  all  headings  to  Notices  and references to legislation are up to date and conform with the Revised Edition of the Laws of Kenya.
texts.text: (iii) Care  should  be  taken  to  ensure  that  all  headings  to  Notices  and references to legislation are up to date and conform with the Revised Edition of the Laws of Kenya.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: EXTRACT FROM THE CODE OF REGULATIONS, SECTION D-
texts.text: EXTRACT FROM THE CODE OF REGULATIONS, SECTION D-

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'D  34.  (1)  Communications  for  the Kenya  Gazette should  reach  the Government  Printer  not  later  than  9  a.m.  on  Friday  of  the  week  before publication is desired. The Government Printer will not publish communications received after that hour until the next subsequent issue of the Gazette.
texts.text: 'D  34.  (1)  Communications  for  the Kenya  Gazette should  reach  the Government  Printer  not  later  than  9  a.m.  on  Friday  of  the  week  before publication is desired. The Government Printer will not publish communications received after that hour until the next subsequent issue of the Gazette.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (2) Ministries will be required to pay for the Kenya Gazette and to meet the cost of advertising in it.
texts.text: Ministries will be required to pay for the Kenya Gazette and to meet the cost of advertising in it.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It  is  emphasized that these notes are for guidance only, but it is requested that  persons  submitting  copy  for  publication  first  satisfy  themselves  that  such copy is complete in every respect.
texts.text: It  is  emphasized that these notes are for guidance only, but it is requested that  persons  submitting  copy  for  publication  first  satisfy  themselves  that  such copy is complete in every respect.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: With effect from 9th August, 2005, subscription and advertisement fee for the Kenya Gazette are as follows:
texts.text: With effect from 9th August, 2005, subscription and advertisement fee for the Kenya Gazette are as follows:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Subscribers and advertisers are advised to remit payment by bankers cheques, Postal  orders  or  money  orders  drawn  in  favour  of  'The  Government  Printer, Nairobi'
texts.text: Subscribers and advertisers are advised to remit payment by bankers cheques, Postal  orders  or  money  orders  drawn  in  favour  of  'The  Government  Printer, Nairobi'

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Revenue stamps cannot be accepted. Subscriptions  and advertisement charges are paid in advance.
texts.text: Revenue stamps cannot be accepted. Subscriptions  and advertisement charges are paid in advance.
